steps.annotation: Loads I/O library so cin and cout exist - without this line cout is undefined
cout works only because <iostream> was included above

teachMe: iostream gives access to cout. The program prints hello.

clozeSentence: To use cout/cin, include the ___ header
explanation: iostream is the C++ standard header for input/output streams cin and cout.

clozeSentence: Headers are loaded with the ___ directive
explanation: #include is a preprocessor directive that pastes the contents of the named header into your file.

spec: Write the single line that loads the I/O library so you can use cin and cout.
explanation: This header brings cin, cout, and endl into scope.

spec: Write a complete program that includes iostream and prints Greetings.
explanation: Standard structure: include header, namespace, main with one cout, return 0.

question: Type the include line for the C++ standard I/O library
Type the two-line header that goes at the top of every SIT102 program

steps.annotation: Now cout, cin, endl, string can be used without std:: prefix
Without using namespace std this would have to be std::cout << x << std::endl

teachMe: using namespace std lets you write cout instead of std::cout. Same output either way.

clozeSentence: Standard library names live in the ___ namespace
explanation: std is the standard namespace. cout, cin, endl, string all live here.

clozeSentence: The keyword that imports a whole namespace is ___
explanation: using namespace X imports all names from namespace X.

spec: Write the line that lets you write cout instead of std::cout.
explanation: using namespace std imports cout, cin, endl etc into global scope.

question: Type the using-namespace line that goes after #include
Type the canonical 2-line preamble used by every SIT102 program

steps.annotation: Entry point - the OS starts your program here. int means it returns an int exit code
First line of main runs first
main must return an int - 0 means success

teachMe: main() is the entry point. Code inside its braces runs when the program starts.

explanation: main returns an int (the program exit code). 0 = success, non-zero = error.

clozeSentence: The OS-required entry function is named ___
explanation: The standard C++ entry point function is main. The OS calls main when running your program.

spec: Write the function header line for main (no body, no return).
explanation: Returns int (exit code), takes no parameters, opens function body.

question: Type the absolute minimum program that compiles and exits successfully
Type the full minimal SIT102 skeleton: 2 header lines plus main returning 0

steps.annotation: Statement ends with ; - declares balance and assigns 100
; ends this cout statement
; ends return statement

teachMe: Each statement ends with ; The program declares x=7 and prints 7.

clozeSentence: Every C++ statement must end with ___
explanation: Forgetting ; is the most common compile error. Every statement requires it.
; tells the compiler the statement is finished. Required after every statement.

spec: Write a single statement that declares an int named score and assigns it 90, properly terminated.
explanation: Declaration and initialization in one statement, ending with ;

spec: Write a 3-statement main body: declare int n=10, print n, return 0. Each statement must end with semicolon.
explanation: Three statements, each terminated by ;

question: Type a return-zero statement
Type a single statement declaring int total = 0, semicolon-terminated

steps.annotation: { marks the start of main's function body
Inside the { ... } block, statements run in order

teachMe: { on line 1 opens main's block. The cout inside runs, prints open.

clozeSentence: After int main() the next character is ___
explanation: { opens the function body block. Every { needs a matching }.
{ marks the start of any code block: function body, if body, loop body.

spec: Write main() that opens a block, prints calculator, and returns 0. Show the opening brace.
explanation: { after int main() opens the function body.

question: Type a main() function header that opens its body block
Type an if-statement opener for condition x > 0 with opening brace

steps.annotation: Last statement before block ends
} closes main's block - matches the { on line 3

teachMe: } on line 4 closes main's block. Without it the program would not compile.

explanation: Every { needs a matching }. Mismatched braces cause compile errors.
} closes the if-block that started with {.

spec: Write a complete program that prints multi-cout. Make sure both { and } are present and balanced.
explanation: Opening { after main(), closing } at the end.

question: Type the closing brace that ends a main() function body
Type a main() with empty body that returns 0 - both braces present

steps.annotation: Last work is done
return 0 tells the OS the program completed successfully

teachMe: After printing done, return 0 ends main and signals success to the OS.

explanation: By convention 0 means success. Non-zero values indicate errors.

clozeSentence: The keyword that exits a function with a value is ___
explanation: return X exits the function, returning X to the caller (or OS for main).

spec: Write the line that ends main with a successful exit code.
explanation: 0 = success. Required final statement of main().

spec: Write a complete program with the canonical return-zero ending. Print the word zero before returning.
explanation: Standard SIT102 program structure ending with return 0;

question: Type the line that ends main with success status
Type the last 2 lines of every SIT102 main(): return zero then close brace

steps.annotation: Comment - compiler ignores everything after //
This actually runs
Another comment - no effect on output

teachMe: Comments are ignored. Only cout statements produce output. Result: yes (not 'not printed').

explanation: // makes the rest of the line a comment ignored by the compiler.

clozeSentence: After // you can write any text - the compiler ignores it
explanation: // starts a comment. Everything after on that line is ignored by the compiler.

spec: Write main() that prints OK with one comment above the cout explaining what it does.
explanation: // adds a comment above the cout. Comment is ignored at runtime.

question: Type a single-line comment saying 'This program prints the score'
Type the canonical file-header comment for a calculator C++ source file

steps.annotation: Extra spaces around tokens are fine - all ignored by compiler
No spaces around << also fine - same meaning

teachMe: All on one line, no spaces. Compiler does not care - prints 8 just the same.

clozeSentence: Spaces around the = operator are optional - the assignment operator is ___
explanation: Whitespace around = is cosmetic. int x=5; and int x = 5; are identical.

clozeSentence: Stream insertion uses ___ - whitespace around it is optional
explanation: << is the stream insertion operator. Spaces around it are not required.

spec: Write a single statement declaring int age=21 with no spaces around the equals sign.
explanation: Whitespace around = is optional. The compiler accepts age=21 just as well as age = 21.

question: Type a one-line main with no extra spaces - declares int n equal to 5 then returns 0
Type cout shifting Hi then endl with no whitespace around <<

steps.annotation: No indent - compiler does not care
Lots of indent - compiler still does not care

teachMe: Indentation is for humans. The compiler ignores it. Output: a then b.

explanation: C++ does not care about indentation. Use it for human readability only.

spec: Write main() that prints two greetings - your indentation can be anything you like.
explanation: Two cout calls, indentation conventional but flexible.

question: Type a main with deliberately weird indentation that prints the word style
Type main with standard 2-space indented body that prints zero
